response.justification: The candidate is a skilled polyglot full-stack developer with genuine software craftsmanship credentials and some distributed systems exposure, but does not meet the core requirements for this role. The most critical gap is C++ experience: it appears only briefly (~6 months) at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and is absent from the skills section entirely, against a mandatory minimum of 8 years. Cloud compute platform depth is also insufficient — AWS CDK usage and Kubernetes deployment of a side project do not constitute production-level cloud architecture experience. Technical leadership is present at a surface level (Staff titles, workshops, mentoring), but not in the cloud or infrastructure domain this role demands.
strengths: Consistent software craftsmanship culture across roles (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, pairing) with evidence of spreading practices through workshops and Refactoring Dojos
Real distributed systems exposure at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second with low-latency async I/O and high-performance data pipelines
Staff-level seniority with community teaching activity (conference talks, multi-day Selenium courses, public workshops) indicating communication and mentoring capability
concerns: C++ experience is approximately 6 months versus the 8-year hard requirement; C++ is not listed in the skills section and there is no evidence of systems-level C++ at scale
No demonstrable ownership of a cloud compute or infrastructure platform; cloud exposure is limited to AWS CDK for a pricing service and Kubernetes for a personal open-source project
Recent employment history shows a current career gap since October 2025 and a very short tenure at re:cinq (6 months), which combined with the profile mismatch makes this a low-probability fit
